Quity Clinic Portal · Design System v1.0

A warm, paper-grade clinical portal.

One source of truth for color, type, surfaces, components, and the rules that hold them together. Every value below is a token. If you find yourself reaching for a hex, stop — there's a token for that, or there should be.

Stack · Next.js 16 · shadcn (base-nova) · Tailwind · MUI X DataGrid v9 Repo · clinic-portal/src/app/globals.css

Contents

  1. Principles
  2. Color
  3. Typography
  4. Spacing & layout
  5. Radii
  6. Shadows
  7. Surfaces
  8. Icons
  9. Tables
  10. Buttons
  11. Inputs
  12. Status badges
  13. Alerts & banners
  14. Dropdowns
  15. Checkboxes & toggles
  16. Expandable buttons
  17. Tabs
  18. Tooltips & popovers
  19. Modal / dialog
  20. Side sheet
  21. Empty states
  22. Loading & skeleton
  23. Avatars
  24. Date picker
  25. Search input
  26. Pagination
  27. Breadcrumbs
  28. Form layout
  29. Filter bar
  30. Toast / snackbar
  31. File upload
  32. Stepper
  33. Tags / chips
  34. Keyboard hints
  35. Cards
  36. Non-negotiables

01 · PRINCIPLES

Five rules, in order.

When a decision is ambiguous, walk these top to bottom.

1Tokens, not values. Every color, radius, shadow, and font reference goes through a CSS variable. New surfaces get new tokens — they don't get hardcoded hexes. If you can't pick a token, the system is incomplete; tell the design lead.
2Calm before contrast. The product is clinical software for staff who use it for hours a day. Tints over fills, hairlines over heavy borders, sentence case over caps. Save the punch for a single primary action per screen.
3One of everything. One button family, one input style, one dropdown component, one table style. If you need a variant, add it to the system — don't fork it on a page.
4Density is fixed. 36px controls. 56px table rows. 44px table headers. 16px gutter inside cards. These don't change to "fit more in" — if a screen is crowded, the screen is wrong, not the density.
5The page color flows through everything that isn't a card. Tables dissolve into the background. Section headers tint just enough to anchor. The card surface (--card) is for things that genuinely sit on top of the page — modals, popovers, sidebar, patient panels.

02 · COLOR

Warm paper, terracotta primary, restrained semantics.

The palette is intentionally narrow. One brand color, four neutrals, six status families. Everything else is built from these.

Surfaces (light mode)

Background
--background
#faf9f5
Card
--card
#f5f4ef
Popover
--popover
#ffffff
Sidebar
--sidebar
#f5f4ee
Muted
--muted
#ede9de
Accent (hover)
--accent
#e9e6dc
Secondary
--secondary
#e9e6dc
Table header
--table-header
#efede3

Brand & ink

Primary
--primary
#c96442
Foreground
--foreground
#3d3929
Muted ink
--muted-foreground
#6e6d68
Border
--border
#dad9d4

Status (badge bg / fg / border)

--status-success-*Linked   Success / linked / completed#dcfce7 / #166534
--status-warning-*Pending   Pending / awaiting#fef9c3 / #854d0e
--status-danger-*Cancelled   Cancelled / failed#fee2e2 / #991b1b
--status-info-*In review   Informational / in progress#dbeafe / #1e40af
--status-accent-*Priority   Priority / highlighted#f3e8ff / #6b21a8
--status-neutral-*Archived   Archived / inactive#f3f4f6 / #4b5563

Feedback (solid surfaces — toasts, snackbars)

Use solid feedback colors only on full-bleed surfaces where the soft status palette would lack contrast. Pair each with its -foreground.

Success
--feedback-success
#16a34a
Info / neutral
--feedback-info
#1f1e1d
Danger
--feedback-danger
#dc2626

03 · TYPOGRAPHY

Outfit for everything. Geist Mono for code.

One sans family, one weight scale (400 / 500 / 600 / 700), one ink color (foreground or muted-foreground). The serif token exists but is unused in the UI — keep it that way unless you have a documented reason.

Page title · h1
24px / 700 / -0.01em
Patients
Card title · h2
18px / 600
Recent activity
Section title · h3
16px / 600
Medical history
Small label · h4
14px / 600
Allergies
Body
14px / 400 / 1.55
A 64-year-old patient presenting with persistent lower back pain, requesting a script renewal for tramadol 50mg. Last consult: 14 days ago.
Small body
13px / 400 / muted
Last updated 30 minutes ago by Dr. Margaret Walsh.
Caption
12px / 400 / muted
Generated email · auto-routed to PMS inbox
Overline
11px / 500 / 0.08em / uppercase
Section header
Stat number
30px / 700 / -0.02em
12,408
Mono
13px / 400 / Geist Mono
PMS-2024-038271

04 · SPACING & LAYOUT

Multiples of 4px. Always.

The base unit is --spacing = 4px. Every gap, padding, and margin in the app is a multiple of it. If you find yourself writing 17px or 23px, round to the nearest 4.

4px·1
8px·2
12px·3
16px·4
20px·5
24px·6
32px·8
48px·12
64px·16

Layout grid

Sidebar widthCollapsed and expanded states240px / 64px
Header heightTop bar with breadcrumb & actions56px
Page paddingOuter gutter on dashboard pages32px
Content max-widthTables are full width; forms cap out1280px
Card paddingInside dashboard cards & panels20px
Field spacingVertical gap between form rows16px

05 · RADII

Rounded, but not rounded-rounded.

Generous corners — the system is built around --radius = 16px and scales up and down from there.

--radius-sm12px · inputs, badges, sub-pills
--radius-md14px · buttons, small cards
--radius-lg16px · cards, dropdowns, popovers
--radius-xl20px · hero cards, sheet headers

06 · SHADOWS

Whisper-soft. Often, none at all.

Most surfaces in the app sit on the page with a 1px border, no shadow. Shadows are reserved for things that genuinely float — popovers, dropdowns, modals, sheets.

--shadow-xsresting cards
--shadow-smelevated cards
--shadow-mddropdowns, popovers
--shadow-lgmodals, sheets

07 · SURFACES

Where each color goes.

The most common cause of off-spec work is "I just used a tinted neutral." Every surface in the app maps to exactly one token. Use this table.

--backgroundApp canvas. Page body. Table rows. Footer of any table.#faf9f5
--cardAnything that genuinely sits on top of the page: dashboard cards, patient detail panels, form sections.#f5f4ef
--popoverFloating: dropdowns, menus, tooltips, modals, sheets.#ffffff
--sidebarLeft navigation rail only. Don't reuse elsewhere.#f5f4ee
--mutedDisabled inputs, ghost button hover, tab-list backgrounds, sidebar item hover.#ede9de
--accentHover states on rows and list items. Selected tab background. Reserved for interaction.#e9e6dc
--secondarySecondary button background. Quiet chip backgrounds. Don't paint surfaces with this.#e9e6dc
--table-headerJust the column header band of every DataGrid in the app. Nothing else.#efede3

08 · ICONS

Lucide. One stroke weight. Three sizes.

Every icon in the portal comes from lucide-react. No mixing libraries, no SVG one-offs. The library is in package.json already — if a glyph isn't in lucide, the answer is usually "use words instead."

Sizes

Three sizes only. Most of the UI uses 16px. Buttons, list items, table cells, dropdown items — all 16px. 14px is for inline-with-12px-text contexts (badges, micro-meta). 20px is for empty-state heroes and the sidebar nav.

14px · size-3.5
16px · size-4 · default
20px · size-5
32px · empty-state hero

Color treatments

Icons take their color from currentColor. Set the color on the parent — never hardcode a fill or stroke.

Muted (default)
--muted-foreground
Active / hovered
--foreground
On primary surface
--primary-foreground or --primary
Destructive only
--destructive

The library — in-product glyphs we actually use

Not exhaustive — these are the icons that appear in the live portal today. Hover the import name to copy. Imported from lucide-react.

LayoutDashboard
User
Users
UserPlus
CalendarDays
FileText
Pill (Heart)
Search
Filter
Plus
X
Check
ChevronRight
ChevronDown
MoreHorizontal
Pencil
Trash2
Download
Upload
Settings
Database
Activity
TrendingUp
AlertTriangle
AlertCircle
Info
CheckCircle2
XCircle
Clock
ShieldCheck
MessageSquare
Phone
Mail
UserCheck
CheckSquare
Menu
ChevronLeft
PanelLeftOpen
RefreshCw
ChevronDownNav

Spec

Librarylucide-react · pinned via package.json. No Heroicons, no Material Icons, no inline SVGs.lucide-react
Stroke weight1.8 (lucide default). Don't override per icon.stroke-width: 1.8
Sizes14 / 16 / 20 / 32. Default 16. Use Tailwind size-* utilities, never width/height in CSS.size-3.5 / 4 / 5 / 8
ColorAlways currentColor. Set the parent's color and the icon follows. No inline fill or stroke colors.currentColor
Spacing from text8px gap (gap-2 in flex). Icon left of label by default.gap-2
Decorative iconsAdd aria-hidden="true" when label exists nearby.aria-hidden
Standalone (icon-only) buttonsAlways pair with aria-label. The visible icon is not a label.aria-label
Filled glyphsDon't. Lucide icons are line-style — keep them line-style. If you need filled, that's a bug.stroked only

09 · TABLES

Two surfaces. One separator.

Tables in this app are content, not containers. The header is the only tinted band. Rows are page-colored. Hairlines do all the structural work.

Name
Email
Date of birth
Status
John Smith
john.smith@gmail.com
14 Mar 1986
Linked
···
Margaret Walsh hover
margaret.walsh@bigpond.net.au
30 Nov 1945
Pending
···
Sam Nguyen selected
sam.nguyen03@gmail.com
20 Sept 2007
Pending
···
Rachel Greene
rachel.greene@outlook.com
2 Jul 1992
Linked
···
header · #efede3 · --table-header row · #faf9f5 · --background hover · #e9e6dc · --accent selected · 8% --primary on --background

Spec

Header heightShorter than rows on purpose44px
Header text12px / 600 / foreground / sentence case (no caps, no tracking)--foreground
Row heightSingle density. No compact toggle.56px
Row text14px / 400 / foreground / centered vertically--foreground
Cell paddingHorizontal only; height controls vertical0 16px
SeparatorHairline below every row. Last row has none.1px --border
HoverWhole row, including the action menu cell--accent
Selected8% primary mixed into background. Subtle.color-mix
Action columnFar right. Hidden by default, reveals on row hover.60px
Checkbox columnFar left. 14×14 checkbox.40px
Date formatDay, three-letter month, four-digit year. No slashes.30 Nov 1945

Variant · zebra + dense (opt-in)

Use only for dense reference tables — audit logs, sync history, raw exports, anything log-shaped where a row's identity is the row itself (not a clickable record). Default DataGrids stay quiet. Combine .zebra with .dense and .log grid for the full effect.

Time
Actor
Event
Result
10:42:18
dr.chen@quity
Updated patient #P-2041 medications
200 OK
10:42:09
system
PBS gateway sync · 14 prescriptions
200 OK
10:41:55
dr.chen@quity
Issued prescription #RX-8821
201 Created
10:41:42
system
PBS gateway sync
502 Bad Gateway
10:41:30
nurse.kelly@quity
Created consultation #C-2042
201 Created
10:41:14
dr.chen@quity
Logged in (SSO · Clerk)
200 OK
10:40:58
system
Cron · expired-script cleanup · 3 records
200 OK
odd row · #faf9f5 · --background even row · #f3f1e8 · --table-row-alt hover · #e9e6dc · --accent
When to useAudit logs, webhook history, raw API responses, anything where rows aren't navigable records.log-shaped only
When NOT to usePatients list, prescriptions list, consultations — any DataGrid where rows are clickable records. Stay quiet.default
Row heightCompresses to 40px so more data fits in view.40px
Even-row tintA tiny step warmer than background. Visible but not loud.--table-row-alt
Hover behaviourSame hover as default tables — overrides the zebra tint.--accent

10 · BUTTONS

One family. Five variants.

All buttons are 36px tall, 12px corner radius, medium weight. Variants change color, never shape or size.

Variants · 36px (default)

Sizes

Spec

Height36px default · 28px small · 32px icon-onlyh-9 / h-7 / size-8
PaddingHorizontal: 16px (default) · 12px (small)px-4 / px-3
Radius12px default · 10px smallrounded-xl / rounded-lg
Type14px / 500 / Outfit--font-sans
Icon16px lucide, gap-2 from labelsize-4
Disabledopacity 0.5, no events. Don't grayscale.opacity-50

11 · INPUTS

Outlined, 36px, 10px corners.

Match button height. Focus ring is a 3px halo of --ring at 40% — soft, not laser-bright.

12 · STATUS BADGES

Pills. Pastel fill, dark ink, matched border.

Six families, one shape. Always reference the --status-* tokens — never bg-green-100 or any Tailwind palette color.

Linked Pending Cancelled In review Priority Archived

13 · ALERTS & BANNERS

Five families. Pastel fill, dark ink, matched border.

Same color families as status badges, scaled up for inline messages. Use the block alert for in-card / in-form messaging. Use the banner strip for full-width app-level notices (page tops, header bars).

Block alerts

New script template available

The PBS authority template has been updated for 2026. Existing drafts are unaffected.

Profile saved

Your prescriber details have been updated.

AHPRA registration expires soon

Your registration expires on 30 June 2026. Renew to keep prescribing.

Prescription submission failed

The PBS gateway returned an error. Check the patient’s Medicare number and try again.

You’re viewing an archived patient

This patient was archived on 12 March 2026. Reactivate to continue care.

Banner strips

Full-bleed, sit at the top of a page or section. No rounded corners.

Scheduled maintenance Sunday 03:00–05:00 AEST. Plan around it. Details
PBS connection lost — e-prescriptions queued. Retry now

Page content sits below the banner.

Inline form errors

For per-field validation. 12px text, destructive ink, sits directly under the input.

Must be 13 characters starting with MED.

Variant usage

InfoNeutral notices, what's new, tips. The default when in doubt.--status-info-*
SuccessConfirm a save / submission completed. Linked / synced records.--status-success-*
WarningReversible problems, expiry warnings, soft errors. Action recommended.--status-warning-*
DangerDestructive results, hard failures, blocked actions. Action required.--status-danger-*
NeutralRead-only states, archived data, info-as-context.--status-neutral-*

14 · DROPDOWNS

One menu component. No exceptions.

Action menus, column menus, filters, view toggles — all use the same shadcn DropdownMenu primitive. MUI's built-in column menu must be replaced via the DataGrid v9 slots API so the table has a single visual vocabulary.

Live examples

View details
Copy email
Delete
Action menu · 220px
Name
Email
Date of birth
Mobile
Location
PMS ID
Status
Toggle menu · 240px

Spec

WidthAction menus 220px · Filter menus 240px · Never wider than 280px220 / 240 / 280
Item heightSame as button height. No 56px rows.36px
PaddingContainer 4px around items · Item 8px horizontalp-1 / px-2
BackgroundPopover surface, with shadow-md and 1px border--popover
Item icon16px lucide, muted ink, 8px gap from labelsize-4
Toggle indicatorRight-side checkmark (primary color) for active items. No left icon when toggle.size-3.5
Separator1px border, 4px margin top & bottom. Only between distinct groups.--border
DestructiveAlways last. Always preceded by a separator. Hover bg = 10% destructive.--destructive
Section headerSkip when there's only one group. Trigger label is enough context.—

15 · CHECKBOXES & TOGGLES

Square for selection. Pill for state.

Checkboxes are 16×16, 4px corners, terracotta when checked. Radios are circles for one-of-many choices. Switches are pill toggles for instant-apply settings.

Checkbox states

Radio

Switch

Spec

Checkbox size16px square, 4px corner radiussize-4
Checkbox border (resting)1.5px solid input ink, on background--input
Checkbox checkedFilled with primary, white tick--primary
IndeterminateFilled with primary, horizontal white bar (used for "select all" partial)--primary
Radio size16px circle, 8px inner dot when selectedsize-4
Switch dimensions36×20 track, 16×16 thumb, 2px inset36×20
Switch onTrack fills primary, thumb slides right--primary
Label gap10px between control and label textgap-2.5
Disabledopacity 0.5, cursor not-allowedopacity-50

16 · EXPANDABLE BUTTONS

Icon at rest. Label on hover.

Used in dense surfaces — table headers, sticky toolbars, patient detail tabs — where every pixel of horizontal space matters. The icon is the handle; the label appears on hover or focus to confirm intent. Hover the buttons below to see the reveal.

Variants

Spec

Resting32×32 circle, 1px border, muted icon, transparent bgsize-8
HoverWidth animates to fit label · 200ms ease · bg fills muted, icon switches to foreground.25s ease
Icon16px lucide, 8px from left edge of pillsize-4
Label14px Outfit medium, 6px gap from icon, 12px right paddingpx-3
Border1px solid border, full pill (rounded-full)--border
Destructive variantBorder & icon flip to destructive on hover, bg = 10% destructive--destructive
When to useTertiary actions in toolbars, table headers, patient detail tabs. Never as a primary CTA.—
When NOT to usePrimary actions, mobile (no hover), forms (use real labels)—

17 · TABS

Pill-on-muted for in-card switching. Underline for page-level.

Two patterns. Pill tabs sit inside cards or panels and switch between sibling content. Underline tabs sit at page level (e.g. patient detail) and switch between major sub-views.

Pill tabs (in-card)

Underline tabs (page-level)

Pill heightIn-card switcher32px
Underline heightPage-level switcher40px
Active indicatorPill: card surface + sm shadow / Underline: 2px primary border-bottomvar(--primary)
Count badgeInactive: muted grey / Active: primary11px / 18px tall

18 · TOOLTIPS & POPOVERS

Dark for hints. Light for content.

Tooltips are tiny dark labels that surface on hover for icon-only controls. Popovers are full content surfaces that open on click — definitions, mini-forms, "what does this mean".

Tooltip — dark, transient

Tooltip · 12px text · 6px radius
Edit patient
Archive (⌘⇧A)

Popover — light, with content

Popover · 280px · 12px radius
What is the PMS ID?

The unique identifier from your Practice Management System. Used to sync demographic and visit data automatically.

19 · MODAL / DIALOG

For confirms, edits-in-place, and "are you sure".

440px max-width. Title + body + foot bar with primary action right-most. Backdrop is 40% black. For destructive actions, the primary button is destructive variant.

Modal · 440px · 16px radius · backdrop rgba(0,0,0,0.4)

Archive patient?

Sarah Chen will be moved to archived patients. You can reactivate her record at any time.

20 · SIDE SHEET / DRAWER

For deep detail without losing the list behind it.

380–520px wide, slides from the right. Use for showing a row's full detail (consultation, note, document) when you don't want a full page navigation. Header has title + close. Footer has primary action right-most.

Side sheet · 380px · slides from right

Consultation #C-2042

Sarah Chen · 24 Apr 2026, 10:30am

Reason for visit

Follow-up for hypertension management. BP elevated last reading.

Notes

Patient reports better adherence to medication. Increased activity. Discussed dietary changes.

21 · EMPTY STATES

Icon · Headline · Body · CTA.

For first-use, zero-results, and post-clear states. Always offer the next action — never a dead end.

No patients yet

Add your first patient to start managing consultations and prescriptions.

No matches for "tylenol"

Try checking spelling, removing filters, or searching by patient name instead.

22 · LOADING & SKELETON

Skeletons for tables. Spinners for buttons. Progress for long jobs.

Tables and cards get skeleton placeholders while loading. Buttons get an inline spinner. Long tasks (uploads, exports) get a determinate progress bar.

Skeleton row

Spinners

Progress bar

Uploading scan_results.pdf · 2.4 MB of 3.8 MB

23 · AVATARS

Pastel fill, dark ink. Initials when no photo.

Use the same six pastel families as status badges. Hash the patient ID to a stable color so the same person always gets the same swatch. Never use the brand terracotta — keep it for actionable surfaces.

Sizes

JL

xs · 24px

SC

sm · 32px

MR

md · 40px

DT

lg · 64px

Color rotation

JL SC MR DT PB AK

With status dot

JL SC MR

Stack (care team)

JL SC MR +4

24 · DATE PICKER

Calendar popover. Single-date and range.

280px wide. Today is bold + primary ink. Selected day is filled primary. Range fills the in-between days with a 15% primary tint.

Single date

April 2026
MoTuWeThFrSaSu 303112345 6789101112 13141516171819 20212223242526 27282930123

Range

April 2026
MoTuWeThFrSaSu 303112345 6789101112 13141516171819 20212223242526 27282930123

25 · SEARCH INPUT

Search icon left. Clear-X and ⌘K hint right.

36px tall like all other inputs. Use ⌘K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) as the canonical global-search shortcut. Clear-X appears only when there's a query.

⌘ K
⌘ K

26 · PAGINATION

"1–25 of 247" left. Page numbers right.

Sits below every paginated table. Always show the range info — users want to know where they are. Show ellipsis if more than 7 pages.

Showing 1–25 of 247 patients
…

27 · BREADCRUMBS

Above the page title. Last item is current.

For pages 2+ levels deep. Skip on dashboard and top-level list pages. Separator is a chevron, not a slash.

Patients Sarah Chen Consultation #C-2042

28 · FORM LAYOUT

Two columns. 16px row gap. 20px column gap.

Labels above inputs. Required fields get a red asterisk. Helper text sits below the input, 12px muted ink. Group related fields into card sections.

Format: YYYY-MM-DD
10 digits including IRN

29 · FILTER BAR

Pills above tables. Active count + clear-all.

Each filter is a pill. Active filters show their value and a remove-X. The "+ Filter" pill opens a menu of fields to filter by. Right side shows result count.

142 results

30 · TOAST / SNACKBAR

Transient confirmations. Bottom-right corner. 4s auto-dismiss.

For "saved", "deleted", "sent" — non-blocking acknowledgements. Stack vertically with 8px gap. Critical errors should use modals, not toasts.

Patient profile saved
3 prescriptions queued for review
Failed to send to PBS gateway

31 · FILE UPLOAD

Dashed dropzone. List of uploaded files below.

Click anywhere on the dropzone to open the picker, or drag files in. Hovering with files shows the active border. Uploaded files appear as rows below with name, size, and remove-X.

Click to upload or drag and drop

PDF, JPG, PNG up to 10 MB

scan_results_2026-04-25.pdf
2.4 MB
Uploaded
referral_letter.pdf
1.1 MB · uploading…

32 · STEPPER

Numbered. Connecting bar fills as steps complete.

For multi-step intake forms or onboarding. Done steps are filled primary. Active step is hollow primary with halo. Future steps are muted.

1Patient details
2Medical history
3Consent
4Review

33 · TAGS / CHIPS

For multi-select inputs and removable categories.

Smaller and quieter than badges. Used inside form fields to represent selected options. Always have a remove-X.

Diabetes Hypertension Asthma

34 · KEYBOARD HINTS

For shortcuts in tooltips, menus, and search hints.

Mono font. 1px border with thicker bottom border for the keycap effect. Use Mac symbols (⌘ ⌥ ⇧ ⌃) by default; we'll handle Windows display swap at runtime.

⌘ K  Global search ⌘ ⇧ N  New patient /  Focus search Esc  Close dialog

35 · CARDS

Card surface, 1px border, 14px radius, soft shadow.

Cards are for content that genuinely sits on the page — dashboard stats, patient panels, form sections. They are not wrappers around tables (tables dissolve into the page).

Active patients

2,847

+12.4% vs last month

Pending consultations

38

14 awaiting prescriber review

36 · NON-NEGOTIABLES

The things we don't litigate per PR.

If any of these show up in a diff, the PR gets reverted. They're not preferences — they're the rails that keep the system intact.

×No hardcoded hexes outside globals.css. Every color reference goes through a token. grep -rE '#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}' src/ outside the tokens file should return zero new hits in any PR.
×No Tailwind palette colors. No bg-blue-500, text-slate-600, border-gray-200. Use semantic tokens — bg-primary, text-foreground, border-border — and the --status-* family.
×No alternating row fills on record tables. No nth-child(even), no zebra on the patients / prescriptions / consultations grids. Tables are quiet. The opt-in .zebra .dense .log variant is only for log-shaped reference tables (audit history, sync logs).
×No uppercase & tracking on table headers. Sentence case. Zero letter-spacing. Always.
×No per-page DataGrid styling. One shared style object, used by every grid. If a screen genuinely needs to differ, it gets a documented variant in the shared file.
×No emoji in product UI. Use lucide icons. (Internal tools like docs, this page, changelogs are fine.)
×No new tokens without sign-off. If you find yourself wanting a new color, ask first. Most "new" needs are an existing token misused.
✓Every UI claim ships with a screenshot. Especially for AI-generated PRs. "Implementation matches spec" is not verification — pixels are.
✓Use the right surface. Page background for tables and the canvas. Card surface for things that sit on top. Popover surface for things that float. When in doubt, look at section 07.
✓One primary action per screen. Multiple terracotta buttons in the same view = pick the most important and demote the rest to secondary or outline.
×No color-only status. A green dot or red pill is never the only signal. Every status has a label, every destructive button has a verb, every error has text. Color is the second channel — never the first.
×No clinical data in toasts. Toasts dismiss in 4s. If the user needs to read a name, dose, MRN, or appointment time to verify their action — use a modal or inline confirmation. "Saved" is fine. "Sent prescription for amoxicillin 500mg to John Doe" is not.
×No destructive actions without a verb. "Delete patient", "Cancel consultation", "Revoke access" — never "Confirm" or "OK" on a destructive modal. The button label is the last thing the user reads before committing.
×No motion over 200ms on UI feedback. Hover, focus, dropdown open, toast slide — all under 200ms. Page-level transitions can go to 300ms. Anything longer feels broken in a clinical workflow where users are clicking through dozens of records.
×No data loss on browser back. If a form has unsaved changes, intercept navigation and confirm. Clinical staff lose work to accidental back-button hits more than any other cause.
×No truncation without a tooltip. If text is cut with ellipsis, the full value must be available on hover. This is non-negotiable for names, MRNs, medication names, and dates.
×No focus rings stripped. Never outline: none without a replacement. The terracotta focus ring is the system's accessibility contract.
✓Empty states earn their keep. Every empty state explains why it's empty and offers the next action. "No consultations yet" is a placeholder; "No consultations yet — book the first one" is a design.
✓Loading states match the shape of what's loading. Skeleton rows for tables. Skeleton cards for cards. Spinners only for buttons and small inline waits. Never a full-page spinner if the layout is knowable.
✓Numbers right-align. Text left-aligns. Dates left-align. Numerical columns (counts, amounts, IDs) are right-aligned with tabular numerals. Everything else is left-aligned. No center-aligned table cells, ever.
✓Dates use a single format per surface. Tables get 14 Jan 2025. Timestamps get 14 Jan 2025, 09:42. Relative times (2h ago) only in activity feeds. Never mix formats in one column.
✓44px minimum hit target on touch surfaces. Even though we're desktop-first, clinicians use tablets at point of care. Icon buttons, row actions, and checkboxes all hit 44×44 minimum.
✓Read the screen aloud. Before merging a new screen, read the headline, primary action, and first row aloud as if to a busy GP. If it doesn't make sense in 5 seconds, the design isn't done.

Quity Clinic Portal · Design System v1.0 · Source of truth: src/app/globals.css
